content:
Steel is a critical material in laser cleaning applications due to its widespread use in industrial components. Its properties dictate laser parameters to avoid thermal damage or surface alteration during oxide and contaminant removal. Below is a detailed table of material properties for carbon steel (e.g., AISI 1018), commonly encountered in laser cleaning of manufacturing equipment. Note that alloy variations (like stainless steel 304) may shift these values slightly, requiring tailored laser settings. Always conduct trials to optimize pulse energy and wavelength for specific steel grades.

| Property                | Value                          |
|-------------------------|--------------------------------|
| Density                 | 7.87 g/cm³                    |
| Melting Point           | ~1425-1540°C                 |
| Thermal Conductivity    | 51.9 W/m·K (at 100°C)        |
| Specific Heat Capacity  | 0.486 J/g·K (at 25°C)        |
| Surface Hardness        | ~126 HB (Brinell, annealed
